This chunk defines the `SyncSession` struct, which manages a synchronization session between two peers. It includes methods for initiating, responding to, and completing the sync process.

This chunk contains tests for the `SyncSession` and related functionalities in a mesh network environment. It includes various test cases to ensure the session's state transitions, message serialization, merging of envelopes, and handling of symmetric differences between two stores.
This Kotlin file contains utility functions for converting ULong values to epoch milliseconds and seconds.

This chunk defines a timing jitter generator for relay operations in Rust. It includes structs, enums, and functions to manage jitter configurations, compute random delays based on the configuration, and validate the configuration.
This chunk contains test functions for JitterConfig serialization and distribution bias.

The TopicManager class manages subscriptions to gossipsub topics in an Android application. It includes methods for initializing, subscribing, unsubscribing, refreshing known topics, auto-subscribing to peer topics, filtering messages by topic, publishing bytes to a topic, checking if subscribed to a topic, and getting all subscribed and known topics.

The code chunk defines a `WebTransportManager` struct that manages WebRTC and WebSocket connections. It includes methods for adding and removing relays, handling incoming messages, queuing outgoing messages, and managing channels.

This chunk contains the implementation of a `WebTransportManager` struct and its associated methods. It includes tests for various functionalities such as adding channels, updating channel states, managing pending outgoing queues, queueing large messages, removing channels, handling connection state transitions, and persisting relays.

The TransportManager class orchestrates all local transports for SCMessenger. It manages BLE, WiFi Aware, and WiFi Direct transports, implements transport priority, auto-escalation when BLE discovers a peer, reports available transports to Rust AutoAdjustEngine, and coordinates peer discovery across all transports.
Provides transport capability discovery and peer registration types.
This file defines the Typography object in the com.scmessenger.android.ui.theme package, which is used to configure the appearance of text components in a Material 3 theme.

This chunk defines the message types and structures used in a messaging application. It includes enums for different types of messages, such as `MessageType`, `DeliveryStatus`, and `Envelope`. The `Message` struct represents a plaintext message before encryption, while the `Receipt` struct represents a delivery receipt. The `Envelope` struct contains all the necessary information to decrypt the message, including the sender's public key, ephemeral public key, nonce, ciphertext, and optional ratchet DH public and message number. The `SignedEnvelope` struct adds an Ed25519 signature for relay verification.
WebSocket Transport Implementation
This chunk contains test functions for the `estimate_bandwidth_from_rssi` and `get_discovered_peers` methods in the `WifiAwareTransport` struct. It also includes a configuration validation test.
This chunk defines the initial setup for a surgical edit graph, including creating a dummy test file and initializing the state.
This module implements a self-healing retry loop for surgical file edits, protected by a strict Philosophy Verifier that ensures all changes comply with core architectural principles.
This chunk is the main entry point of the `swarm.py` script, handling command-line arguments and executing the tasks.
This class manages the initialization and operations of a mesh repository, including logging message delivery attempts, detecting retry storms, initializing managers, and performing migrations.
This code defines a method to start the mesh service with a given configuration.
This class manages various state and data structures used in the Mesh repository, including peer tracking, message delivery tracking, and retry logic.

This code handles various events related to peer discovery and message processing in a mesh network. It updates discovered peers, emits identity and connected status changes, persists route hints, and manages contacts based on received messages.
This code defines a class that handles incoming messages and updates the mesh service state.
This class manages the BLE identity beacon and handles peer identity reads.
This class handles various mesh-related operations such as starting the mesh service, sending delivery receipts, and managing identity and history syncs.
This class manages mesh services and transports, including BLE, WiFi, and Swarm.

This class provides various methods to interact with the Mesh service, including checking if a peer is blocked, listing blocked peers, getting blocked count, signing data, verifying signatures, managing device IDs, exporting logs, and handling message history.

This class manages the mesh service and its associated components, including history management, ledger operations, and network connectivity.

funcs.name: resetAllData
calls_out_to: context.filesDir.listFiles()?.forEach { file -> if (file.isDirectory) file.deleteRecursively() else file.delete() }

funcs.name: replayDiscoveredPeerEvents
calls_out_to: _discoveredPeers.value.forEach { (mapKey, info) -> canonicalPeerId = info.peerId.trim().ifEmpty { mapKey.trim() } if (canonicalPeerId.isEmpty()) return@forEach normalizedKey = normalizePublicKey(info.publicKey) aggregateKey = normalizedKey ?: canonicalPeerId routeCandidate = when { PeerIdValidator.isLibp2pPeerId(mapKey) -> mapKey PeerIdValidator.isLibp2pPeerId(canonicalPeerId) -> canonicalPeerId else -> null } discoveredNickname = prepopulateDiscoveryNickname(nickname = info.nickname, peerId = canonicalPeerId, publicKey = normalizedKey) existing = aggregates[aggregateKey] if (existing == null) { aggregates[aggregateKey] = ReplayDiscoveredIdentity(canonicalPeerId = canonicalPeerId, publicKey = normalizedKey, nickname = discoveredNickname, localNickname = info.localNickname, routePeerId = routeCandidate, transport = com.scmessenger.android.service.TransportType.INTERNET) } else { if (existing.publicKey.isNullOrBlank() && !normalizedKey.isNullOrBlank()) existing.publicKey = normalizedKey existing.canonicalPeerId = selectCanonicalPeerId(canonicalPeerId, existing.canonicalPeerId) existing.nickname = selectAuthoritativeNickname(discoveredNickname, existing.nickname) if (existing.localNickname.isNullOrBlank() && !info.localNickname.isNullOrBlank()) existing.localNickname = info.localNickname if (existing.routePeerId.isNullOrBlank() && !routeCandidate.isNullOrBlank()) existing.routePeerId = routeCandidate if (existing.transport != com.scmessenger.android.service.TransportType.INTERNET && info.transport == com.scmessenger.android.service.TransportType.INTERNET) existing.transport = info.transport } aggregates.values.forEach { peer -> listeners = peer.routePeerId?.let(::getDialHintsForRoutePeer).orEmpty() publicKey = peer.publicKey if (!publicKey.isNullOrBlank()) emitIdentityDiscoveredIfChanged(peerId = peer.canonicalPeerId, publicKey = publicKey, nickname = peer.nickname, libp2pPeerId = peer.routePeerId, listeners = listeners) else com.scmessenger.android.service.MeshEventBus.emitPeerEvent(PeerEvent.Discovered(peerId = peer.canonicalPeerId, transport = peer.transport)) }

summary: This class provides various functionalities related to mesh operations, including exporting diagnostics asynchronously, managing identity emissions, and applying settings.
This class provides methods for managing mesh communication, including setting BLE scan interval, relay budget, updating device state, and handling peer connections via SwarmBridge.

funcs.name: ensurePendingOutboxRetryLoop
calls_out_to: repoScope.launch { while (true) { try { primeRelayBootstrapConnections() flushPendingOutbox("periodic") kotlinx.coroutines.delay(8000) } catch (e: kotlinx.coroutines.CancellationException) { throw e } catch (e: Exception) { Timber.w(e, "Pending outbox retry loop error") kotlinx.coroutines.delay(5000) } } }

funcs.name: ensureCoverTrafficLoop
calls_out_to: repoScope.launch { while (true) { try { kotlinx.coroutines.delay(30_000) val enabled = try { settingsManager?.load()?.coverTrafficEnabled == true } catch (_: Exception) { false } if (enabled) { val core = ironCore val bridge = swarmBridge if (core != null && bridge != null) { try { val payload = core.prepareCoverTraffic(256u) bridge.sendToAllPeers(payload) } catch (e: Exception) { Timber.d("Cover traffic send skipped: ${e.message}") } } } } catch (e: kotlinx.coroutines.CancellationException) { throw e } catch (e: Exception) { Timber.w(e, "Cover traffic loop error") kotlinx.coroutines.delay(30_000) } } }

funcs.name: startMeshService
calls_out_to: launch { MeshEventBus.peerEvents.collect { event -> when (event) { is PeerEvent.Connected -> { connectedPeers.add(event.peerId); updateNotification(); NotificationHelper.showPeerDiscoveredNotification(context = this@MeshForegroundService, peerId = event.peerId, transport = "Connected") } is PeerEvent.Disconnected -> { connectedPeers.remove(event.peerId); updateNotification() } else -> {} } } }
launch { MeshEventBus.statusEvents.collect { event -> when (event) { is StatusEvent.StatsUpdated -> { messagesRelayed.set(event.stats.messagesRelayed.toInt()); updateNotification() } else -> {} } } }
